page: Microsoft Azure F48ads_v7 is a virtual machine instance from the Fadsv7 family, powered by an x86_64 AMD EPYC 9V45 processor. It features 48 dedicated physical cores operating at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz, with 1.0 thread per core. The hardware configuration includes 192.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Additionally, the instance is equipped with 5669 GB of local NVMe storage and is Premium Storage capable, though it does not include any GPU accelerators or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance across multiple compute and memory metrics. The F48ads_v7 achieves top 10% placement in stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests, as well as PassMark CPU and physics evaluations. Memory performance is highly competitive, with small-block cached read bandwidth and large-block write bandwidth both ranking in the top 10%. Database operations and Redis SET operations also perform in the top tier, with Redis reaching 135.87 million operations per second.

While the instance excels in CPU-bound tasks and database operations, its performance scales down on larger workloads. LLM inference benchmarks show top-tier and strong results for small and medium models, but drop to average tier for large 70B models due to the absence of dedicated GPU hardware. This resource profile makes the F48ads_v7 highly cost-efficient for high-throughput database hosting, static web serving, data compression, and medium-scale machine learning inference, where dedicated high-frequency CPU cores and substantial local storage are required without the premium cost of GPU acceleration.
description: Microsoft Azure F48ads_v7 is an x86_64 virtual machine from the Fadsv7 family, powered by a 4.5 GHz AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with 48 dedicated physical cores and 192.0 GB of RAM. This configuration features a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core and includes 5669 GB of local storage. Benchmark results place the instance in the top 10% for both single-core and multi-core CPU performance, as well as database operations and Redis SET throughput. However, without GPU acceleration, LLM inference performance drops to average for larger 70B models. The F48ads_v7 offers a cost-effective, high-performance profile for compute-heavy workloads, database hosting, and web serving that benefit from high clock speeds and fast local storage without requiring GPU hardware.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F48ads_v7 is a 48-core AMD EPYC virtual machine with 192 GB RAM and 5669 GB local storage, delivering top-tier CPU and database performance for demanding enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure F48ads_v7 is a 48-core AMD EPYC virtual machine with 192 GB RAM, 5.6 TB local storage, and top-tier CPU performance.
tagline: A high-frequency, dedicated AMD EPYC server featuring substantial local storage and top-tier compute performance for database and web workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 physical cores running at 4.5 GHz
192.0 GB of system memory and 5669 GB of local NVMe storage
Top-tier CPU, memory bandwidth, and database benchmark performance
Average performance on large-scale LLM inference due to lack of GPU
Best suited for database hosting, web serving, and high-throughput compute tasks
